description: Lock Public Art Academies to Renaissance art instruction in drawing, anatomy, geometry, and perspective rather than conservation workshops.

checks.reason: The node remains anchored to the Florentine academy founded in 1563.
Perspective and geometry remain central to the scoped academy curriculum.
Anatomy is the corrected source-backed teaching prerequisite.
Anatomical study enables academy figure drawing and instruction without being a physical component.
Conservation workshops should not be restored unless the node is narrowed to conservation training institutions.
